original_query: SELECT "t2"."EMPNO", "t2"."ENAME", "t2"."JOB", "t2"."MGR", "t2"."HIREDATE", "t2"."SAL", "t2"."COMM", "t2"."DEPTNO", "t2"."SLACKER", "t3"."DEPTNO" AS "DEPTNO0", "t3"."NAME" FROM (SELECT "EMPNO", "ENAME", "JOB", "MGR", "HIREDATE", "SAL", "COMM", "DEPTNO", "SLACKER" FROM "EMP" ORDER BY "SAL" FETCH NEXT 10 ROWS ONLY) AS "t2" LEFT JOIN (SELECT * FROM "DEPT") AS "t3" ON "t2"."DEPTNO" = "t3"."DEPTNO" ORDER BY "t2"."SAL" FETCH NEXT 10 ROWS ONLY
rewritten_query: SELECT "t2"."EMPNO", "t2"."ENAME", "t2"."JOB", "t2"."MGR", "t2"."HIREDATE", "t2"."SAL", "t2"."COMM", "t2"."DEPTNO", "t2"."SLACKER", "t3"."DEPTNO" AS "DEPTNO0", "t3"."NAME" FROM (SELECT "EMPNO", "ENAME", "JOB", "MGR", "HIREDATE", "SAL", "COMM", "DEPTNO", "SLACKER" FROM "EMP" ORDER BY "SAL" FETCH NEXT 10 ROWS ONLY) AS "t2" LEFT JOIN (SELECT * FROM "DEPT") AS "t3" ON "t2"."DEPTNO" = "t3"."DEPTNO" ORDER BY "t2"."SAL" FETCH NEXT 10 ROWS ONLY

rewrite_suggestion.produced_suggestion: Removed unnecessary subquery for DEPT table by directly joining with the DEPT table
Removed redundant FETCH NEXT 10 ROWS ONLY from the outer query since the inner query already limits to 10 rows
Used more meaningful table aliases (e and d) instead of t2 and t3 for better readability
